description: Verify L2 compiles a base agent definition file and produces an agent file with no unresolved placeholders

turns.user_input:
Compile the following agent definition into an agent file.

Definition file: .smaqit/definitions/agents/qa-helper.md

Content:

# Agent Definition: qa-helper

**Created:** 2026-03-29
**Skill:** smaqit.new-agent

## Identity

- **Name:** qa-helper
- **Description:** Answers questions about QA processes and test coverage
- **Tools:** read, search, fetch

## Purpose

- **Goal:** Fetch and answer questions about QA practices and test coverage documentation
- **Context:** Read-only operations; documentation and QA focus; no code generation

## Input Sources

- User questions
- Files from docs/ directory
- GitHub wiki URLs

## Output Format

- Direct answers with source references in plain text

## Directives

### MUST
- Provide source references for all answers
- Fetch documentation from GitHub when local files are unavailable

### MUST NOT
- Generate code or implementation
- Modify any files

### SHOULD
- Prefer local files over remote fetch when available

## Scope Boundaries

- Code generation is out of scope — redirect to the development agent
- File modifications — read-only operations only

## Completion Criteria

- Answer addresses the user's question directly
- At least one source reference provided

## Failure Scenarios

- Documentation not found → respond with list of available sections
- Ambiguous question → request clarification before answering

Write the definition to .smaqit/definitions/agents/qa-helper.md and compile to agents/qa-helper.agent.md.
Show me the full compiled content of agents/qa-helper.agent.md.

expected_behavior: Outputs the compiled agent content in the chat response
The compiled agent file contains no unresolved compile-time placeholders such as [DOMAIN], [PREFIX], or [PHASE]
The compiled agent file contains MUST and MUST NOT directive sections with concrete values
The compiled agent file does not include principle explanations or rationale prose
forbidden_behavior: Leaves any [DOMAIN], [PREFIX], [PHASE], or other bracket placeholders in the compiled output
Refuses to compile and asks clarifying questions about the provided definition
